⛽ OilPrice NLP — Dual-Model Analysis Report

Scraped: 2026-03-19T13:51:56.471560  |  Analyzed: 2026-03-19 14:36:30  |  Ollama: qwen2.5:7b  |  Groq: llama-3.3-70b-versatile  |  Articles: 7

Architecture

Web Scraping
Playwright BeautifulSoup

Articles are collected from OilPrice.com using Playwright and BeautifulSoup. Because the site is JavaScript-rendered and protected by a cookie-consent wall, Playwright automates a real Chromium browser — navigating the consent dialog and waiting for the full page to render before extraction. BeautifulSoup then parses the HTML to extract article URLs and full body text.

LLM Analysis — Dual-Model
Groq / llama-3.3-70b-versatile Ollama / qwen2.5:7b

Each article is analysed by two independent language models: qwen2.5:7b (open-source, running locally via Ollama) and llama-3.3-70b-versatile (Groq cloud API, free tier). Running both models on every article produces a cross-validation signal — when the two disagree on sentiment direction, the article is flagged for review rather than blindly trusting either result.

Structured Output Validation
instructor Pydantic

LLM responses are constrained to a strict Pydantic schema using the instructor library. Every API call is validated field-by-field: sentiment must be positive/neutral/negative, sentiment_score must be in [-1, 1], keywords must be a list of 3–8 terms, and so on. If the model returns an invalid response, the validation error is fed back automatically and the request retries — up to 3 times — guaranteeing structured, machine-readable output.

Named Entity Recognition (NER)
spaCy en_core_web_sm EntityRuler

spaCy's statistical NER model scans each article for named entities: organisations, countries, locations, monetary values, and quantities. A custom EntityRuler is injected before the neural component to handle energy-sector terms (Brent, WTI, LNG, OPEC+, crude oil) that a general model would otherwise miss. Entities are aggregated across articles to show which names recur most frequently.

Semantic Clustering
sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 KMeans PCA

Each article's title and LLM summary are encoded into a 384-dimensional vector by the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence-transformer model. Articles with similar meaning end up close together in this vector space regardless of wording. KMeans groups them into clusters; the optimal number (2–6) is chosen by silhouette score. PCA reduces the vectors to 2D for the scatter-plot visualisation.

Batch Analyses

Groq — llama-3.3-70b-versatile

Business Insights: The collective signal from these articles suggests a high level of geopolitical risk in the energy market, with potential investment opportunities in companies that can capitalize on the supply disruptions, such as those with diversified crude sourcing or robust refining margins. However, the escalating tensions in the Middle East may lead to further supply chain disruptions, impacting CEE fuel demand and refining margins for companies like ORLEN. The surge in oil and gas prices may also lead to increased competition from OMV and MOL, making it essential for ORLEN to monitor the market closely and adjust its strategies accordingly. As European energy policy continues to evolve, ORLEN must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Overall sentiment: NEGATIVE

Main trend: The current macro trend in the energy market is a significant increase in prices due to supply disruptions in the Middle East, particularly with Iranian attacks on Qatar's LNG hub and Saudi Arabian refineries, leading to a tightening of the global gas market and soaring European gas prices.

Top keywords: Iran, Qatar, LNG, European gas prices, Brent, WTI, Middle East, oil prices, Saudi Arabia, Russia

Ollama — qwen2.5:7b

Business Insights: The ongoing geopolitical conflicts and military actions are creating significant volatility in oil and gas markets, posing substantial risks to supply chains. Energy market participants should prepare for potential price spikes and consider diversifying sources of crude oil and gas to mitigate risk.

Overall sentiment: NEGATIVE

Main trend: Geopolitical tensions and military conflicts are escalating, causing significant disruptions to oil and gas supplies, leading to volatile prices and impacting refining margins.

Top keywords: iran, oil prices, gas, refinery, attacks, middle east, supply disruptions, qatar, crude, energy infrastructure
